rules.description: Trigger must be a native <button> element
rules.rationale: Native button provides keyboard accessibility (Enter/Space), implicit role=button, and disabled semantics without additional ARIA.
Screen readers announce the expanded/collapsed state to users.

rules.description: Trigger must have aria-controls referencing the panel id
rules.rationale: Programmatically links trigger to its controlled panel.

rules.description: Panel must have role=region and aria-labelledby
rules.rationale: Marks the panel as a landmark region labeled by its trigger for screen reader navigation.
Icon is decorative — screen readers must not announce it.

rules.description: Every trigger class must have a corresponding CSS rule
Component CSS must not use primitive tokens directly in selectors
rules.rationale: Component CSS must speak component-level semantic tokens.

rules.description: No hardcoded hex, rgba, or pixel values in component selectors
exceptions: Custom property fallback values in :root or .accordion {} token bridge block are permitted
rules.rationale: All visual values must use CSS custom properties to support theming.

rules.description: Focus indicator must be visible — :focus-visible must not be removed without replacement
rules.rationale: 2px solid focus ring required per Figma spec and accessibility standards.

rules.description: Disabled trigger must not show hover or pressed backgrounds
rules.rationale: Disabled controls must not respond to pointer interaction.

rules.description: Flush accordion parent must have overflow:clip or overflow:hidden
rules.requirement: Parent element must establish a containing block with clipping
rules.rationale: Flush interaction background bleeds via negative inset — parent must clip or bleed will be visible outside bounds.

rules.description: Title element must be a <span> with class .accordion__title
rules.rationale: Heading semantics (h1-h6) are provided by the surrounding page context, not by the accordion title element itself.

rules.description: All horizontal spacing must use CSS logical properties
rules.rationale: CSS logical properties ensure automatic RTL support via parent dir=rtl.

rules.description: Icon rotation transition should respect prefers-reduced-motion
rules.rationale: Users who prefer reduced motion should not see rotation animation.

rules.description: Component-level tokens should be added to token.css
rules.rationale: Centralizing tokens in token.css enables theming and consistent overrides.
